acceptance_criteria: A docker-compose.yml exists at the repo root that brings up the Django app (and any required services). README documents 'docker compose up' as the launch command. The app is reachable at localhost after compose up.
description: The M6 extra credit milestone explicitly requires the Django application to be deployed via Docker Compose. The whispers/ Django app is functionally complete but there is no Dockerfile or docker-compose.yml anywhere in the project. Without these, the M6 requirement is only partially satisfied (7/10 in current grading).
title: Add Docker Compose for M6 Django deployment

acceptance_criteria: POST /api/tts/{video_id} produces a WAV where each segment's duration matches the source timestamp window. The TTS model is not reloaded between calls. The endpoint runs in a thread pool to avoid blocking.
description: Expose POST /api/tts accepting video_id and target_language. Use tts_es.py as the service layer but with the timestamp synchronisation fix from beads issue foreign-whispers-projects-ccu applied: per-segment audio generation with pyrubberband time-stretching and silence padding. Return the path to the generated WAV. TTS model should be loaded at startup, not per-request.
title: Port TTS + audio-sync endpoint to FastAPI

acceptance_criteria: The credentials file requirement is documented in the README with instructions for obtaining it. A .gitignore entry exists for the file. The script degrades gracefully (falls back to youtube-transcript-api) when the credentials file is not present.
description: download_video_captions.py requires a credentials_desktop_app.json file for Google OAuth authentication against the YouTube Data API. This file is not included in the repository (correctly, as it contains secrets), but there is no .env.example, no documentation of what the file must contain, and no fallback to the simpler youtube-transcript-api flow when the credentials file is absent. Anyone attempting to run the script gets an immediate FileNotFoundError.
title: OAuth credentials file missing from submission

acceptance_criteria: POST /api/transcribe/{video_id} triggers transcription without blocking the event loop. The response (or polled status) contains segments with start/end/text. The Whisper model is not reloaded between calls.
description: Expose POST /api/transcribe accepting a video_id. Run Whisper transcription via transcribe.py service layer using the model loaded at startup (not re-loaded per request). Return structured segments JSON. Since transcription is long-running, the endpoint should either run in a thread pool (run_in_executor) or return a job_id immediately and expose GET /api/transcribe/{job_id}/status for polling.
title: Port transcription endpoint to FastAPI

acceptance_criteria: translate_all_files() (or its caller in app.py) only translates the specific video being processed. Previously translated files are not reprocessed.
description: translate_all_files() in translate_en_to_es.py iterates over every JSON file in the transcriptions_en/ directory and overwrites the corresponding file in transcriptions_es/. When the Streamlit app processes a new video, this function retranslates all previously processed videos unnecessarily. For 10 videos this adds significant latency and wastes argostranslate inference. The function should accept a specific file path or video ID to scope its work.
title: translate_all_files() reprocesses entire directory on every run

acceptance_criteria: All file paths are derived from a single configurable base directory (e.g., a constant at the top of app.py or an env variable). No path literals are duplicated across files.
description: Multiple files assume a fixed directory layout relative to the script working directory: stitch_video_with_timestamps() in translated_output.py constructs paths like './ui/raw_video/', './ui/translated_audio/', etc. The video_cached() check in the Django app (whispers/vid2vid/views.py) uses a hardcoded BASE_PATH. app.py also uses inline string literals for all intermediate directories. Moving any file or running from a different working directory causes silent failures or FileNotFoundErrors.
title: Hardcoded file paths break portability

acceptance_criteria: A YouTube link to the pitch video is present in the README under a clearly labelled section.
description: M5a awards 5 points for a 30-second pitch video uploaded to YouTube with a link in the submission. milestone6_demo.webm exists locally in the project directory but there is no YouTube upload and no link in the README. The 5 points are at risk.
title: Add pitch video YouTube link to README

acceptance_criteria: POST /api/download with a valid YouTube URL returns {video_id, transcript_segments[]}. Calling it twice for the same URL skips re-download. Invalid URLs return 422.
description: Replicate the functionality of whispers/vid2vid/views.py download logic as a FastAPI router. Expose POST /api/download accepting a YouTube URL and returning video_id plus the downloaded transcript as JSON. Use the existing download_video.py and download_video_captions.py modules as the service layer — do not duplicate their logic. Add the re-download guard from beads issue foreign-whispers-projects-fo6 as part of this work.
title: Port video download and caption endpoint to FastAPI

acceptance_criteria: POST /api/translate/{video_id} translates only that video's transcript. Previously translated videos are not reprocessed. Response contains translated segments with timestamps preserved.
description: Expose POST /api/translate accepting video_id and target_language. Use translate_en_to_es.py as the service layer. Fix the scope bug from beads issue foreign-whispers-projects-5ss (translate only the specified video, not the entire directory) as part of this work. Return translated segments JSON.
title: Port translation endpoint to FastAPI

description: The current M6 extra credit implementation uses Django (whispers/ app) to serve the translation pipeline. Django is heavyweight for this use case: it brings ORM, admin, sessions, and auth middleware that are unused. FastAPI is a better fit — async-native (important since Whisper, TTS, and video processing are all long-running), automatic OpenAPI docs, and lighter to containerise for the Docker Compose requirement. This epic covers replacing the whispers/ Django app with a FastAPI backend while keeping app.py (Streamlit) as the frontend.
title: Migrate M6 backend from Django to FastAPI

acceptance_criteria: Each TTS segment is stretched/compressed to match the duration of its source timestamp window. Silent padding is inserted for gaps between segments. The final WAV is assembled by concatenating in timestamp order. Dubbed video is audibly in sync with lip movement.
description: tts_es.py generates a single concatenated WAV file by joining all translated segments end-to-end at the TTS model's natural speaking rate. There is no per-segment time-stretching or silence padding to align speech to original speaker timestamps. When the dubbed audio is laid over the video, timing drifts immediately and compounds across the full duration. The correct approach is to generate each segment as a separate audio file, use pyrubberband.time_stretch() to compress or expand it to fit its target timestamp window, insert AudioSegment.silent() for gaps between segments, then concatenate in order to produce the final WAV.
title: TTS output has no audio-video timestamp synchronisation

acceptance_criteria: Before downloading, the script checks whether the target MP4 and caption JSON files already exist. Existing files are skipped with a log message.
description: download_video() in download_video.py does not check whether a video file already exists before calling pytube to download it. Running the script twice will re-download all 10 videos, overwriting existing files. This is slow and unnecessary. The youtube-transcript-api caption download has the same issue.
title: download_video.py re-downloads videos on every run

acceptance_criteria: POST /api/stitch/{video_id} produces a dubbed MP4 with burned subtitles. GET /api/video/{video_id} streams it with correct Content-Type. ImageMagick presence is validated at startup with a clear error if absent.
description: Expose POST /api/stitch accepting video_id to produce the final dubbed video via translated_output.py. Expose GET /api/video/{video_id} to stream the output MP4 using FileResponse. Fix the ImageMagick dependency issue from beads issue foreign-whispers-projects-ttl and the hardcoded path issue from foreign-whispers-projects-6e1 as part of this work.
title: Port video stitching and serving endpoint to FastAPI

acceptance_criteria: docker compose up starts both api and ui services. Streamlit UI at :8501 can submit a YouTube URL and the FastAPI backend at :8000 processes it end-to-end. Shared media volume is correctly mounted. GET /docs on the API service returns the OpenAPI UI.
description: Replace the Django-based Docker Compose requirement (beads issue foreign-whispers-projects-012) with one for the FastAPI app. Write Dockerfile for the FastAPI service and docker-compose.yml at the repo root with two services: api (FastAPI/uvicorn) and ui (Streamlit app.py). Mount a shared volume for media files so both services can access downloaded videos and generated outputs. Update README with 'docker compose up' instructions.
title: Add Docker Compose for FastAPI deployment

acceptance_criteria: A public Hugging Face Space exists running app.py. The Space URL is recorded in the project README. A user can paste a YouTube URL and receive a translated video without running anything locally.
description: M5a of the spec explicitly requires the Streamlit UI to be deployed on Hugging Face Spaces (Streamlit Spaces). app.py is a complete Streamlit implementation but it only runs locally. No HF Space exists. This is a graded milestone requirement worth 15 of the 20 M5a points.
title: Deploy Streamlit app to Hugging Face Spaces

acceptance_criteria: uvicorn main:app --reload starts without errors. GET /healthz returns 200. OpenAPI docs are available at /docs. Whisper and TTS models are loaded once in the lifespan context.
description: Create the FastAPI entry point (main.py) with app instantiation, CORS middleware, and lifespan hooks for loading the Whisper model and TTS model once at startup (rather than per-request as the current Django views do). Establish the directory layout: routers/, services/, schemas/ — mirroring the existing Django apps but without ORM machinery. Add fastapi, uvicorn[standard], and python-multipart to requirements.txt.
title: Define FastAPI app skeleton and project layout

acceptance_criteria: requirements.txt or a setup script installs or validates ImageMagick. README documents the system dependency with the install command for common platforms. moviepy IMAGEMAGICK_BINARY is set correctly at startup.
description: stitch_video_with_timestamps() in translated_output.py uses moviepy TextClip objects, which require ImageMagick to be installed system-wide. ImageMagick is not listed in requirements.txt and is not mentioned anywhere in the README or documentation. The app fails at runtime with a cryptic moviepy error when ImageMagick is absent. Additionally, moviepy must be configured with the correct ImageMagick binary path via IMAGEMAGICK_BINARY.
title: Add ImageMagick as explicit dependency and document install
